long: 1 · Manual craft (do it well by hand)

themes.title: Styles: the document's skeleton
notes: styles over manual formatting; heading hierarchy
rungs: Design the style set for the document you describe
Apply consistent styles across this draft, in place
Build a styled document from a plain-text draft

themes.title: Structure & navigation
notes: nav pane, TOC, breaks and sections done right
rungs: Plan the section structure for the document you describe
Insert and update the table of contents; reorganise via headings
Restructure a document file to a given outline by script

themes.title: Tables & figures
notes: tables that behave; captions; figures that stay put
rungs: Choose table vs figure vs prose for the content you describe
Format this table consistently; add captions throughout
Generate the document's tables from the data files

themes.title: Review & collaborate
notes: track changes, comments, compare — the GDM reality
rungs: Triage this list of reviewer comments: accept, push back, or discuss
Summarise what changed in this reviewed document
Build a compare-and-report pass across document versions

themes.title: Templates & consistency
notes: document templates; theme fonts/colours; building blocks
rungs: Turn the document you describe into a template checklist
Create a new document from the organisation's template
Build the organisation's document template from a brand spec

lessons.title: Styles, not formatting: fix it once
The heading hierarchy: one outline, three payoffs
Breaks & sections: why your page numbering fights you
The table of contents that maintains itself
Tables that behave: widths, headers, repeat rows
